content_gaps: The specific chemical processes or techniques jewelers use to create an antique finish (e.g., liver of sulfur, specific oxidizing agents). Most pages are vague, just saying chemicals are used.
A clear visual comparison (photos/videos) showing the difference between a polished, matte, and antique finish on the same base metal.

Detailed guidance on how the 'antique' finish wears over time and what to expect. Many pages discuss natural patina developing over 100+ years, but not how a deliberately applied finish evolves with wear.
The distinction between a true antique piece (100+ years old) and a modern piece with an 'antique-style' finish is often blurred. A dedicated section clarifying this for buyers is needed.
How different base metals (bronze, silver, brass, gold) take an antique finish differently in terms of final color and texture.
